User Story: As a visitor, I want a clear empty converter so I know how to begin.
Expected Behaviour: Input is empty; character count is 0; Convert is disabled; Preview is hidden.

User Story: As a visitor, I want typed or pasted markdown to activate conversion.
Expected Behaviour: Character count matches the raw input length; Convert enables only when trimmed input is non-empty; Preview appears.

User Story: As a visitor, I do not want whitespace-only content treated as a document.
Expected Behaviour: Convert remains disabled and Preview remains hidden for whitespace-only input.

User Story: As a visitor, I want an immediate preview of my markdown.
Expected Behaviour: Preview refreshes after every input event using the current Beautify and theme settings.

User Story: As a visitor, I want optional styled output.
Expected Behaviour: Checking Beautify applies styled preview and styled DOCX generation; unchecking restores the plain document preview.

User Story: As a returning visitor, I want my theme preference restored.
Expected Behaviour: Saved localStorage theme wins; otherwise the OS dark-mode preference is used.

User Story: As a visitor, I want to switch light and dark themes.
Expected Behaviour: Theme button toggles data-theme, persists light/dark in localStorage, and refreshes the preview palette.

User Story: As a visitor, I want to upload a markdown or text file.
Expected Behaviour: Selecting a .md/.txt/.markdown file loads its text, updates the count, enables Convert, clears the picker, and shows Preview.

User Story: As a visitor, I want visual feedback when dragging a file over the editor.
Expected Behaviour: Drag enter adds the dragging state; leaving the editor removes it without flicker when moving within the editor.

User Story: As a visitor, I want to drop a markdown or text file into the editor.
Expected Behaviour: Dropping the first file removes drag feedback and loads it using the same behavior as file picker upload.

User Story: As a user, I want Markdown H1-H6 headings preserved.
Expected Behaviour: ATX headings with 1-6 hashes render as matching HTML headings and Word Heading 1-6 styles.

User Story: As a user, I want normal text preserved as paragraphs.
Expected Behaviour: Every non-empty non-block line becomes a paragraph in Preview and DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want emphasis preserved.
Expected Behaviour: Bold, italic, bold-italic, underline-style bold, and strikethrough markdown render correctly in plain Preview and DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want inline code visually distinct.
Expected Behaviour: Backtick code renders as code in Preview and as a Consolas shaded run in plain DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want Markdown links to remain usable.
Expected Behaviour: Links render as anchors in Preview and as visually styled link text in DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want fenced code blocks preserved.
Expected Behaviour: Code fences preserve escaped source text and line breaks; optional language appears in styled Preview; DOCX uses monospace shaded lines.

User Story: As a user, I want quoted material distinguished.
Expected Behaviour: Consecutive > lines form one blockquote with line breaks in Preview and indented quote styling in DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want bullet lists preserved.
Expected Behaviour: Consecutive -, *, or + list lines render as a UL and bullet paragraphs in DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want numbered lists preserved.
Expected Behaviour: Consecutive numbered lines render as an OL and sequential numbered paragraphs in DOCX.

User Story: As a user, I want pipe tables preserved.
Expected Behaviour: A pipe header followed by a divider becomes a bordered table; escaped pipes remain cell text; first-row column count defines the DOCX grid.

User Story: As a user, I want section breaks preserved.
Expected Behaviour: Three or more -, *, or _ characters render as an HR and a DOCX thematic/bordered break.

User Story: As a user, I want escaped Markdown punctuation treated literally.
Expected Behaviour: Backslash-escaped markdown punctuation displays without triggering inline formatting.

User Story: As a user, I want feedback while conversion runs.
Expected Behaviour: Clicking Convert shows Converting…, adds loading state, and disables the button until completion or failure.

User Story: As a user, I want a downloadable Word document.
Expected Behaviour: Successful conversion downloads converted.docx, revokes the object URL, changes label to Download, and re-enables the button.

User Story: As a user, I want a clear failure instead of a stuck control.
Expected Behaviour: Failure alerts Conversion failed with the error, restores Convert label, removes loading, and re-enables the button.

User Story: As a dark-mode user, I want a readable preview without creating a dark Word document.
Expected Behaviour: Preview follows dark mode; DOCX generation always uses the light output palette.

User Story: As a visitor, I want to inspect the source repository.
Expected Behaviour: GitHub link opens the configured repository in a new tab without opener access.

User Story: As an extension user, I want formatting enabled by default.
Expected Behaviour: Content script and popup default enabled when storage has no value.

User Story: As an extension user, I want supported AI content recognized.
Expected Behaviour: Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini HTML signatures are identified; semantic structured HTML is labeled html; markdown patterns are labeled markdown.

User Story: As a Google Docs user, I want ordinary plain text left alone.
Expected Behaviour: Empty and non-markdown plain-text clipboard content is not prevented or reformatted.

User Story: As an extension user, I want AI wrappers removed while semantic formatting remains.
Expected Behaviour: Cleaner preserves headings, paragraphs, breaks, quotes, emphasis, lists, safe links, code, and tables; drops scripts, controls, media, and embeds.

User Story: As an extension user, I do not want copied internal anchors in Docs.
Expected Behaviour: Links without href or with # href are unwrapped; external href values are retained and quote-escaped.

User Story: As an extension user, I want plain-text Markdown formatted before insertion.
Expected Behaviour: Headings, code, quotes, lists, tables, rules, paragraphs, inline formatting, and links convert to semantic styled HTML.

User Story: As an extension user, I want cleaned content inserted using the best available browser path.
Expected Behaviour: Extension writes HTML/plain clipboard variants and attempts native paste first.

User Story: As an extension user, I want formatting inserted if native paste is unavailable.
Expected Behaviour: When native clipboard/paste throws, extension attempts execCommand insertHTML with cleaned HTML.

User Story: As an extension user, I want recovery guidance if automatic insertion fails.
Expected Behaviour: When both insertion strategies throw, cleaned clipboard content is retained when possible and a temporary Ctrl+V nudge is shown.

User Story: As an extension user, I want one formatting action per paste.
Expected Behaviour: While processing, duplicate frame events are ignored; lock releases 150 ms after the attempt finishes.

User Story: As an extension user, I want to see formatting usage.
Expected Behaviour: Each recognized paste increments count and records the detected source in sync storage.

User Story: As an extension user, I want the popup to reflect stored settings and stats.
Expected Behaviour: Popup loads enabled/count/lastSource; shows Active or Paused, localized count, friendly source label, and manifest version.

User Story: As an extension user, I want to pause and resume formatting.
Expected Behaviour: Changing the toggle persists enabled state, updates status styling/text, and broadcasts SET_ENABLED to all open Google Docs document tabs.

User Story: As an extension user, I want open document tabs to honor popup changes immediately.
Expected Behaviour: Content script updates its in-memory enabled flag on SET_ENABLED messages.

User Story: As an extension user, I want to open the source repository.
Expected Behaviour: Clicking the popup GitHub link prevents default navigation and opens the configured repository in a new tab.

User Story: As a user, I want the extension limited to Google Docs documents.
Expected Behaviour: Content script only runs on https://docs.google.com/document/* and has only the declared Docs host access.
